key_departures.impact: Dowdle posted back-to-back 1,000-yard rushing seasons (1,373 yards, 7 TDs in 2025) and was the focal point of the Panthers' ground game. Signed a 2-year, $12.25M deal with Pittsburgh. Chuba Hubbard and Trevor Etienne are now the depth options. Clear starter-level departure.
Starting center departed on a 3-year, $25M deal ($14M guaranteed) with Detroit. Left a glaring hole at center that the Panthers had to address in free agency immediately.

Backup LT plan retired despite a 2-year offer from the Panthers. This escalated the OT need created by Ikem Ekwonu's ruptured patellar tendon (suffered in the Wild Card loss vs. Rams). Left the Panthers without a reliable LT option entering free agency.

Released for cap savings of $10.5M. Started 33 games over two seasons with the Panthers (65 tackles, 2.5 sacks in 2025). Was a rotational starter alongside Derrick Brown. Cap casualty, now signed by a division rival. Leaves IDL depth thinner.
Rotational edge rusher with 7.5 sacks over two seasons not expected to return. His departure compounds the EDGE need but is partially mitigated by the Phillips signing.

Was pushed into an every-down role in 2025 due to linebacker attrition and underperformed. Unrestricted FA, remains unsigned. His departure creates no new need given Lloyd's arrival but reduces linebacker depth.
Posted a career-high 111 tackles and started all 17 games in 2025. Departed as a UFA and remains unsigned. Significant starter-level loss at safety — leaves a genuine hole at the position.
Veteran guard/center departed as a UFA. Compound loss alongside Cade Mays thinning the interior line. Depth/injury concern.

signings.analysis: Top-of-market EDGE signing. Phillips received a $35M signing bonus with 2026 and 2027 salaries fully guaranteed — $60M fully guaranteed at signing. At $30M AAV he ranks among the top-8 EDGE contracts leaguewide. Ranked 9th in QB pressures in 2025 (73 pressures) despite only 5 sacks. GM Dan Morgan explicitly targeted pass rush as the team's biggest need. This is the Panthers' guy for 4 years. EDGE drops from a tier-1 critical need to a depth/developmental need. They may still want a EDGE developmental pick in later rounds to pair opposite Phillips alongside 2025 2nd-rounder Nic Scourton, but the need as a high-priority draft concern is fully resolved.

2025 Pro Bowl, Second-Team All-Pro linebacker. $25M guaranteed is real investment — effectively 1.7 years of fully guaranteed money on a 3-year deal, signaling a true starter commitment. Lloyd had 81 tackles, 5 INTs (including a 99-yard pick-six), and career-best pass-rush production in 2025. Morgan explicitly promised to 'attack' the linebacker position and delivered on the best available. LB goes from a tier-1 critical need to tier-3 depth. The Panthers still have Trevin Wallace alongside him, and may draft depth, but the hole that plagued them all of 2025 is genuinely filled.

Ranked as the top LT available in free agency per USA Today's Nate Davis. Started 48 of 52 games over 3 years with the Packers. One-year deal at $10M (well below starter-market AAV of $12-20M) signals a bridge starter while Ekwonu recovers from patellar tendon rupture. Walker is the projected Week 1 LT starter. However, the one-year structure means OT remains a medium-term need — Ekwonu's future is uncertain, Walker is gone after 2026, and Taylor Moton's contract runs through 2027 at RT. Panthers will likely still draft a developmental OT, though the immediate 2026 crisis is averted. OT drops from tier-1 critical to tier-2 moderate need.

Depth/swing tackle with familiarity with HC Dave Canales from their time together in Seattle. Signed before Walker arrived as insurance. With Walker now in hand, Forsythe is the swing tackle/backup. One-year, near-minimum deal. Has 27 starts over 5 seasons — PFF ranked him 72nd of 84 qualifying OTs in 2025. Provides OT depth but doesn't change the need picture on its own.

Signed to replace Cade Mays at center after Mays departed for Detroit. Fortner started 10 games for the Saints in 2025, ranking 17th of 37 qualifying centers per PFF — solid short-term option. His strong suit is pass blocking (allowed only 3 sacks in 2025). However, one-year deal at well below starter IOL AAV ($10-16M range) signals a bridge solution, not a long-term answer. ESPN noted the Panthers will likely still draft a center to develop as an eventual replacement. IOL need drops from tier-1 acute (post-Mays departure) to tier-2 moderate — addressed for 2026 but long-term C is still a draft priority.

Signed as backup QB to Bryce Young, replacing 38-year-old Andy Dalton who is expected to be released or traded. One-year deal ($4M guaranteed) — this is a backup role, not a starter competition. Pickett (27, former 2022 first-rounder) provides experienced depth but this doesn't change the QB starter situation. Bryce Young is QB1 entering his third season. QB need remains essentially unchanged — Young is the clear starter, and Pickett is an upgrade over Dalton as a capable backup.

Former Alabama teammate of Bryce Young; signed for near-minimum money. Projects as WR4 behind Tetairoa McMillan, Jalen Coker, and Xavier Legette. Only 73 career catches across three seasons. One-year deal with $1M guaranteed is the definition of a low-risk flier on familiarity and upside. Does not address WR as a need — this is pure depth at minimum cost. WR need is unchanged.

Re-signed depth/special teams CB. Played 17 games in 2025, accounting for 37.7% of all ST snaps. Filled in for Jaycee Horn at times. One-year deal at minimal value — this is depth and special teams continuity, not a starter acquisition. CB need (if any) is unchanged.
Re-signed primarily as a special teams contributor. Depth CB only. No material impact on positional need.

Re-signed versatile LB/S hybrid. Appeared in 18 games with 1 start in 2025. Can provide safety depth following Nick Scott's departure, but is not a like-for-like replacement for a 17-game starting safety. One-year deal at depth money — S need is only minimally addressed. Panthers still need a true starting safety.

Re-signed restricted free agent. Key rotational DE — started 9 games in 2024 when Derrick Brown was out. Provides interior-exterior line depth. RFA tender, so Panthers retain him on a one-year basis. Depth signing — does not change the EDGE need picture materially (Phillips handles that).
Re-signed primarily as a special teams contributor. Appeared in 18 games with 1 start in 2025. Depth/ST only.
Re-signed veteran WR for depth and leadership. One-year minimum-range deal. Not a starter — depth and locker room presence. No need impact.
Re-signed RFA linebacker. Started 4 games in 2025 plus the Wild Card game, key special teams contributor. Depth/ST role only.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Jaelan Phillips signing (4yr/$120M, $80M guaranteed, $60M fully guaranteed at signing) is a franchise-level commitment at EDGE. At $30M AAV he's among the top-8 EDGE deals leaguewide. Phillips plus 2025 2nd-rounder Nic Scourton give the Panthers a legitimate starting pair. EDGE is now a luxury/developmental day-3 pick at most, not a draft premium.

Devin Lloyd (3yr/$45M, $25M guaranteed) was the best LB in free agency — 2025 Pro Bowl, Second-Team All-Pro. Dan Morgan explicitly 'attacked' this need and landed the target. Wallace remains as a complementary piece. LB is no longer a meaningful draft need in early rounds.

Rasheed Walker (1yr/$10M) is a legitimate starting LT who started 48 of 52 games for Green Bay. Resolves the 2026 crisis created by Ekwonu's patellar tendon tear and Nijman's retirement. However, one-year structure means 2027 is still unsettled — Ekwonu's recovery is uncertain, Walker is a free agent after 2026, and Moton (RT) is aging. Draft a developmental OT to project long-term. Tier drops from urgent-1 to moderate-2.

Luke Fortner (1yr/$4.75M) patches the starting C vacancy created by Cade Mays' departure to Detroit. Fortner was solid in 2025 (17th of 37 centers, PFF) but is on a bridge deal. Center remains a priority need in the draft for long-term planning. Still need to monitor Austin Corbett situation (also unsigned). IOL drops from tier-1 acute to tier-2 moderate.

Nick Scott (career-high 111 tackles, 17-game starter) departed as a UFA and remains unsigned. Isaiah Simmons re-signed on a minimum-range deal but is a depth/hybrid player, not Scott's replacement as a full-time starter. Safety need remains at tier 2 — one of the clearer remaining gaps heading into the draft. Caleb Downs, Dillon Thieneman mentioned as first-round fits.

Rico Dowdle (1,373 yards, 7 TDs in 2025) signed a 2yr/$12.25M deal with Pittsburgh. Chuba Hubbard, Trevor Etienne, and potentially Jonathan Brooks (health permitting) remain. Hubbard has starting experience but is not a proven 300-carry bell cow. Dowdle's departure escalates RB from a non-need to a legitimate draft target in rounds 3-5.

A'Shawn Robinson cut (cap savings $10.5M) and signed by division rival Tampa Bay. Derrick Brown, Tershawn Wharton, Bobby Brown III, and Cam Jackson remain. Depth is thinner with Robinson gone — especially given Wharton's injury history. IDL moves up as a mid-round draft priority to replenish rotation depth.

No meaningful upgrade at WR in free agency. Tetairoa McMillan, Jalen Coker (ERFA tender), Xavier Legette, and Brycen Tremayne return. John Metchie ($1.9M, 1yr) adds depth/familiarity but is not a starter. WR remains a mid-round need — a slot/speed receiver or true WR2 upgrade would still be welcomed in rounds 2-4.

No TE signings. Tommy Tremble led Panthers TEs with only 249 receiving yards in 2025. James Mitchell is a restricted FA. TE remains a genuine need, especially for a receiving weapon. Panthers were identified as a potential target for elite TE free agents pre-FA but did not act. Still a viable round 2-3 draft target.

key_additions.headline: Top free-agent EDGE rusher lands on a franchise deal — 73 QB pressures in 2025 finally fixes the Panthers' chronic pass-rush problem
2025 Pro Bowl, Second-Team All-Pro linebacker (5 INTs, 81 tackles) addresses the Panthers' most glaring defensive weakness in one swing
Top available LT on the market steps in at left tackle while Ikem Ekwonu recovers from torn patellar tendon
Bridge starter at center fills the hole created by Cade Mays' departure to Detroit
Former first-round pick upgrades backup QB room as younger, more athletic insurance behind Bryce Young

key_losses.headline: Back-to-back 1,000-yard rusher departs, leaving a backfield hole that Chuba Hubbard and Trevor Etienne must now fill
Starting center exits for a multi-year deal in Detroit, triggering the Luke Fortner bridge signing
17-game starting safety (career-high 111 tackles) departs, leaving a meaningful hole at the back of Carolina's secondary
Veteran starting DT cut for cap space and signs with a division rival — thins an IDL rotation that now leans heavily on Derrick Brown
Backup LT plan retires despite a multi-year offer, accelerating the team's pursuit of Rasheed Walker
